title: The Clockmaker's Tremor

chart.cc: Progressive hand tremor for 14 months. I can no longer wind the city's great clock.
chart.hpi: 14-month history of rest tremor, right hand predominant. Worsens at rest, improves with intentional movement. Gradual onset, slowly progressive. Shuffling gait, difficulty initiating movement. Treated for depression x8 months without improvement.
chart.fh: No family history of movement disorder.
chart.sh: Retired clockmaker. Lives with husband. Non-smoker. Occasional wine.

tests.name: TSH
tests.result: TSH 1.8 mIU/L — normal. Hyperthyroidism ruled out.

tests.name: MRI Brain
tests.result: No mass lesion. No infarct. Mild age-appropriate cortical atrophy. Standard sequences do not show midbrain abnormality. DaTscan preferred for confirmation.

tests.name: DaTscan
tests.result: Reduced dopamine transporter uptake in bilateral putamen — asymmetric, right greater than left. Consistent with nigrostriatal dopaminergic degeneration.

tests.name: EMG / NCS
tests.result: Normal nerve conduction. No peripheral neuropathy. Tremor: 4-6 Hz rest tremor pattern — consistent with Parkinson disease.

hints: The tremor frequency is 4-6 Hz. Note when it disappears — and when it returns.
Look at her face carefully. A masked expression and reduced blink rate are not depression.
The sertraline did not help the depression. That itself is a clue.
Pill-rolling, cogwheeling, micrographia. The diagnosis is in the examination.

options.feedback_correct: Correct. This is not an emergency. A calm, thorough approach.

options.feedback_wrong: Thiamine is for Wernicke — not indicated here.
Urgent, yes. Emergent, no. This has been progressing for 14 months.
Abrupt SSRI discontinuation causes withdrawal syndrome. Taper if stopping.

options.feedback_correct: The gold standard. You know your pharmacology.

options.feedback_wrong: Haloperidol blocks dopamine receptors — it will dramatically worsen Parkinson disease. Never.
The depression was a misdiagnosis. Sertraline will not help the tremor.
Propranolol is for essential tremor — an action tremor. This is a rest tremor.

options.feedback_correct: Correct. Movement disorder specialists optimize PD management.

options.feedback_wrong: LP is not indicated in Parkinson disease. This is a clinical diagnosis.
Brain biopsy for Parkinson disease would be unconscionable.

options.feedback_correct: Also correct — OT is essential in PD management.
Exactly right. Stable patient, chronic condition, outpatient management.

options.feedback_wrong: No indication for admission. This is a chronic, stable outpatient condition.
The diagnosis is clear. Withholding treatment serves no one.
DaTscan is an outpatient procedure. No need for admission.

explanation: Parkinson disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder caused by dopaminergic neuron loss in the substantia nigra. Cardinal features: rest tremor (4-6 Hz, pill-rolling), bradykinesia, rigidity (cogwheeling), postural instability. Levodopa/carbidopa remains the most effective symptomatic treatment. Dopamine-blocking agents (typical antipsychotics, metoclopramide) are contraindicated — they worsen motor symptoms dramatically.
